name: Cane Sugar
reason: A simple carbohydrate that provides "empty" calories. While safe in small amounts, frequent consumption can lead to blood glucose spikes and contributes to caloric surplus, increasing the risk of metabolic dysfunction over time.

name: Natural Flavors
reason: These are flavor precursors derived from food sources but are often highly processed in a lab. While generally recognized as safe (GRAS), the specific chemical constituents are often proprietary, making it difficult to track individual sensitivities.

name: Soy Lecithin
reason: An emulsifier used to improve texture. It is safe for the majority of the population, but it is often highly refined and derived from genetically modified crops, leading some to limit intake in favor of less processed alternatives.

name: Erythritol
reason: A sugar alcohol used as a low-calorie sweetener. It is generally well-tolerated, but high doses can cause gastrointestinal distress (bloating/laxative effects) and some recent studies suggest monitoring its impact on cardiovascular markers.

name: Partially Hydrogenated Oils (Trans Fats)
reason: These are synthetic fats created through industrial hydrogenation. There is a strong scientific consensus that they raise LDL (bad) cholesterol and lower HDL (good) cholesterol, significantly increasing the risk of heart disease.

name: High Fructose Corn Syrup (HFCS)
reason: A highly processed sweetener linked to increased rates of hepatic lipogenesis (fatty liver), insulin resistance, and obesity. Its rapid metabolism by the liver makes it more detrimental than standard sucrose.

name: Red 40 (Allura Red AC)
reason: A synthetic azo dye derived from petroleum. It has been linked in various peer-reviewed studies to hypersensitivity reactions and potential behavioral disruptions in children, offering no nutritional value while posing unnecessary risks.
